name: Intercom
description: Customer messaging, helpdesk, and product support platform (inbox, conversations, users, and automation).

auth_methods.note: Intercom supports OAuth 2.0 for public apps and Personal Access Tokens (Bearer tokens) for server-to-server/private apps; requests use Authorization: Bearer <token>.

credential_access.note: Intercom’s Developer Hub is where you configure an app and obtain credentials: public apps provide a Client ID/Client Secret (OAuth 2.0), and private apps can generate an access token (personal access token) for API requests.

api_surface.note: Intercom exposes a broad REST API (users, leads, conversations, companies, events, tags, etc.) and also provides a GraphQL endpoint (https://api.intercom.io/graphql) as noted in the API reference.

buildability.note: Public docs plus self-serve credential creation in the Developer Hub allow building integrations today. (Enterprise-only product features may still require paid plans or Sales contact, but API credentials can be obtained from a workspace.)
